nodes.cmd: uip or jobs list --folder-key {{shared_folder_key}} --state Pending --output json

credential_message.regex: (wrong machine credentials|username of the machine is not the same as the username in the user credentials)

when.value: wrong machine credentials|username of the machine is not the same

children.judgment_q: Does the job's error message describe a machine/user credential mismatch (assigned robot account username does not match the host machine user), as opposed to a license/slot shortage?

nodes.cause: Assigned robot account's stored username does not match the host machine's configured user (credential mismatch). The code fires while Orchestrator compares an assigned robot account against machine users — license is NOT the bottleneck, and 'no robot assigned to the folder' cannot be confirmed via CLI (record under open_gaps, never report as confirmed). Job has no StartTime — fault is at dispatch before any robot session.
Machine template has zero Unattended runtime slots allocated (UnattendedSlots: 0) — TemplateNoLicense.

children.judgment_q: Are tenant-level Unattended runtime licenses actually exhausted (Used >= Allowed across templates, not just the assigned template's own allocated slot)? Note: for RobotNoMatchingUsernames, license is never the bottleneck — Used==Allowed reflects the template's allocated slot, not exhaustion.

nodes.cause: Tenant-level Unattended runtime licenses exhausted — no free Unattended slot to dispatch the job.

nodes.reason: Job is Pending with a PendingReason / error code not in the playbook table (not RobotNoMatchingUsernames or TemplateNoLicense, no credential-mismatch message). Read the full job Info and PendingReasons block, and reason from the verbatim message before concluding.
